---
title: "The architecture of pigmentation genetics"
subtitle: "One coupled pigmentation–hormone gene network, pointed at two questions — human association grading, and the evolution of primate sexual dichromatism"
description: "A model-system project on the genotype→phenotype map for pigmentation: a clade-wide test showing sexual dichromatism has a heterogeneous, per-origin genetic architecture across primates, and a convergence method grading how context-dependent human pigmentation associations are."
toc: false
---

::: {.callout-important}
## The headline finding
**Sexual dichromatism in primates has no single genetic basis.** Where the same trait is nearly
a one-gene switch in birds (*MC1R*; [Nadeau et al. 2007](https://doi.org/10.1098/rspb.2007.0174)),
in primates it **arose ~15 times independently** and is built
by **different genes in different lineages** — a heterogeneous, origin-specific architecture. This
is the first clade-wide, multi-gene selection test of the genomic basis of primate sexual
dichromatism. **→ [Read the finding](dichromatism.qmd)**
:::

This project builds **one thing** — a curated, coupled pigmentation gene network (with its
physically-linked sex-hormone axis) — and points it at two questions. Pointed at *human*
genetics, the network grades how much to trust a claimed variant→phenotype link by making
independent evidence layers converge. Pointed at *evolution*, the **same network** yields a
genuine discovery: primate sexual dichromatism is not one gene but a **coupled
pigmentation–hormone system remodeled at different points in different lineages** — which is why
the trait arose ~15 times independently and is so readily lost. Same object, same
one-gene-is-not-enough lesson, two scales.

```{=html}
<iframe src="interactive/substrate_multilayer.html"
        title="Interactive 3D multilayer view of the pigmentation gene substrate"
        loading="eager"
        style="width:100%; height:720px; border:1px solid #e8e2d6; border-radius:10px; background:white;">
</iframe>
```

::: {style="text-align:center; color:#5c656b; margin-top:0.5rem; font-size:0.96rem;"}
**Our integration, not any single source.** The harmonized substrate in three dimensions — four
independent evidence layers, one gene core, with each gene sitting *only* on the layers that carry it.
Drag to rotate · hover a gene.
:::

```{=html}
<div style="text-align:center; margin:1rem 0 0.3rem;">
  <a href="https://pigmentation-gene-network.vercel.app/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"
     style="display:inline-block; padding:0.75rem 1.5rem; font-weight:600; font-size:1.05rem;
            color:#fff; background:#2f6d7a; border-radius:999px; text-decoration:none;
            box-shadow:0 6px 18px rgba(47,109,122,.35);">
    Explore the full immersive network ↗
  </a>
  <div style="font-size:0.9rem; color:#5c656b; margin-top:0.55rem;">
    The full six-layer substrate — all 803 genes, dark, with fly-to on click — opens in a new tab.
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Start here
Short on time? Read the **[dichromatism finding](dichromatism.qmd)** first — it is the discovery:
sexual dichromatism arose ~15 times independently across primates and is built by different genes
in different origins, tested with per-origin RELAX, per-branch aBSREL, and RERconverge.

Then, for the human-pigmentation method, follow **[NB1 · Reconstruct the network](notebooks/01_reconstruct_published_network.ipynb)** → **[NB4 · Unified association base](notebooks/04_unified_association_base.ipynb)** → **[NB10 · Mechanism→direction law](notebooks/10_mechanism_direction_law.ipynb)** → **[NB12 · Direction law, expanded](notebooks/12_direction_law_expanded.ipynb)**. NB10 shows a functional screen ordering the *direction* of Mendelian pigmentation disorders (22/22, permutation p < 1×10⁻⁵); read it with NB12, which extends the result to 29/33 and locates its failure boundary. The human arm is a **methodological** demonstration of the convergence thesis on well-characterized pigmentation biology. Every code cell is folded — click *"Show code"* to expand it.
:::

## What this is

One curated pigmentation gene network — including its physically-coupled sex-hormone axis —
applied to two questions that make the same point: **the map from genotype to pigmentation is not
one-gene-one-trait; you have to read the genes as a coupled network.**

**Across primates (the discovery).** Sexual dichromatism — a trait that is nearly a single-gene
switch in birds — turns out to have **arisen ~15 times independently** in primates and to be built
by **different pigmentation × sex-hormone gene combinations in different origins**. The context
that changes the genetic basis is the evolutionary lineage. **[See the finding →](dichromatism.qmd)**

**Within humans (the method).** To know how much to trust a claimed variant→phenotype link, make
independent lines of evidence converge — statistical association, mechanistic pathway knowledge,
and experimental validation. Applied to human pigmentation, this grades reported associations and
names the next step that would confirm each; it shows the same variant can be causal in one
population and silent in another (context = ancestry), and that the *direction* of an effect is
predictable from mechanism.

Pigmentation is the model system because it is highly heritable, has near-zero environmental
variance, has a well-characterized molecular mechanism, and shows these context effects in a
visible, checkable form — from a single human variant read across ancestries to a whole trait
reassembled across a primate radiation.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Everything here — every notebook, table, and figure — was built during the hackathon event window
(first commit **2026-07-09**). Each notebook is a **self-contained mini-manuscript** (intro → methods →
results → discussion, with citations) that renders from its own stored outputs, so the site needs no kernel,
no network, and no withheld data. **Code is folded by default** — click *"Show code"* on any cell to see it.
:::

## One network, two questions

- **Pointed at evolution — [sexual dichromatism across primates](dichromatism.qmd)** (the
  discovery): using the coupled pigmentation–hormone network to test selection across ~15
  independent origins, and finding the architecture is heterogeneous — different modules
  remodeled in different lineages. Pipeline and cluster protocol in
  [`comparative-genomics/`](https://github.com/tinalasisi/pigmentation-gene-network/tree/main/comparative-genomics).
- **Pointed at human genetics — the convergence method** (the notebooks below): using the same
  network to grade how much to trust human pigmentation genotype→phenotype claims by making
  independent evidence layers converge.

## Building the network, and reading it in humans

### Foundation — the network
- **[NB1 · Reconstruct the network](notebooks/01_reconstruct_published_network.ipynb)** — rebuilds the
  [Raghunath et al. 2015](https://doi.org/10.1186/s13104-015-1128-6) melanogenesis model as a
  **signed, directed** network, typing and signing every
  interaction from the published file, with a resolvable citation per edge.
- **[NB2 · Resolve to genes](notebooks/02_resolve_network_to_genes.ipynb)** — resolves entities to genes
  (UniProt / HGNC / Ensembl / GRCh38) and validates every edge against OmniPath as a separate check; a closing
  gate fails the build if anything is uncited.
- **[NB3 · Validation cases](notebooks/03_assemble_validation_cases.ipynb)** — pulls published genotype→phenotype
  discordance cases from curated papers, counted paper-by-paper, reproduced from committed extracts.

### Networks & substrate
- **[NB4 · Unified association base](notebooks/04_unified_association_base.ipynb)** — a claim-faithful
  effector-status re-read of 105 curated loci: **14 genuinely effector-uncertain** (the rescue target;
  34 with Kim 2024 folded in) vs 75 canonical-effector variant-gaps — correcting an earlier coarse
  "author-unexplained" tag that overstated the open set ~6×.
- **[NB5 · Compare candidate networks](notebooks/05_compare_candidate_networks.ipynb)** — *"the network you
  choose changes the answer"*: how STRING drift and source choice move which genes are in play.
- **[NB6 · Gene-regulatory network](notebooks/06_gene_regulatory_network.ipynb)** — 58 curated, signed TF→target
  regulon edges (MITF / PAX3 / SOX10), every edge cited.
- **[NB7 · Harmonized substrate](notebooks/07_harmonized_substrate.ipynb)** — merges all layers into one tagged
  node/edge table with per-edge provenance and tier.

### Rescue screen
- **[NB8 · Rescue diagnostic](notebooks/08_rescue_screen_diagnostic.ipynb)** — the pivot: the network
  rescue yielded no novel effector; the real result is that **melanocyte-vs-bulk eQTL correction retracts false
  causal-gene calls** — the tissue you resolve in changes the answer.
- **[NB9 · Bajpai orphan reconciliation](notebooks/09_bajpai_reconciliation.ipynb)** — a symmetric STRING seed
  reconnects **93 of 142** CRISPR "orphans" to the core (vs 0/142 under a curated-only seed) — *seeding changes
  the answer*; the residue is the knowledge gap.

### Direction & ancestry
- **[NB10 · Mechanism→direction law](notebooks/10_mechanism_direction_law.ipynb)** — a functional melanin screen
  orders the *direction* of Mendelian pigmentation disorders: positive regulator + loss-of-function allele →
  hypopigmentation (**22/22**, base rate 54%, p < 1×10⁻⁵), robust to the study-bias confound.
- **[NB11 · Cross-ancestry conditionality](notebooks/11_cross_ancestry_conditionality.ipynb)** — some
  pigmentation genes are discovered through *different, population-private variants* in different ancestries,
  quantified with Hudson F~ST~ against a genome-wide baseline.
- **[NB12 · Direction law, expanded](notebooks/12_direction_law_expanded.ipynb)** — a pre-registered expansion
  (**29/33**) that also *locates the failure boundary*: the misses act through systemic routes.

### Sex hormones & evolution
- **[NB13 · Sex-hormone layer](notebooks/13_sex_hormone_layer.ipynb)** — layers the **sex-hormone axis** onto the
  pigmentation substrate through cited **hormone → melanocyte-receptor → MITF** bridges, each gated on melanocyte
  expression (Zhang eQTL). The estrogen bridge runs through membrane **GPER** (not ERα — melanocytes are
  ERα-negative), converging on the *same* cAMP→CREB→MITF node as melanocortin. A separate, additive layer:
  **NB1–7 are unchanged.**

### Appendix — data extraction
Reproducible pulls of each external dataset, frozen for provenance:
[NB1a · Bajpai CRISPR screen](notebooks/01a_extract_bajpai_crispr.ipynb) ·
[NB1b · Baxter gene list](notebooks/01b_extract_baxter_genes.ipynb) ·
[NB1c · HIrisPlex markers](notebooks/01c_extract_hirisplex_markers.ipynb) ·
[NB1d · GWAS Catalog pull](notebooks/01d_reproduce_gwas_catalog.ipynb).

## Reproducibility

Every figure and number renders from committed outputs; external pulls are frozen under
`data/external/db_responses/` with provenance sidecars. The authoritative build log is
[`internal/CHANGELOG.md`](https://github.com/tinalasisi/pigmentation-gene-network/blob/main/internal/CHANGELOG.md);
the full repository is on [GitHub](https://github.com/tinalasisi/pigmentation-gene-network).
